↩️ Reply to ResistanceTrench mirror: "🇮🇷⚡️🇺🇸🇮🇱 Today, IRGC Navy targeted the Abraham Lincoln in the northern Indian Ocean with 4 Qadr 380 cruise missiles. In the second phase of this intelligence and offensive operation, the secret gathering place of flight engineers and American fighter pilots…" 🇮🇷 Get to know the missile Iran reportedly used to attack a U.S. aircraft carrier. IRGC announced late Thursday that Iran fired four Qadr missiles at the Abraham Lincoln aircraft carrier. The Qadr missile is a family of Iranian medium-range ballistic missiles with the following features: - Range of 1650 to 2,000 kilometers. - A powerful explosive warhead of 650 to 1,000 kilograms, or a cluster warhead configuration to turn interception into a nightmare. - Launch weight of 15 to 19 tons. - Distinctive triangular nose cone to improve speed and reentry accuracy (CEP 30 to 50 meters for some variants). Inertial and satellite guidance are standard. - First stage liquid fuel and second stage solid fuel. Preparation time: about 30 minutes. - Can be launched from Iran’s extensive underground missile cities or mobile road TEL trucks. - Developed in the mid-2000s from the older liquid-fueled Shahab-3. - Widely used by Iran in a series of retaliatory attacks called the True Promise 2024, 2025, and 2026. Deploying this missile against the Lincoln carrier may indicate new upgrades – including a dynamic guidance system with tracking and terminal maneuver capability, additional radar or infrared guidance systems, and/or installation of specialized anti-ship munitions to penetrate ship armor and explode below the waterline for maximum damage. The Qadr missiles are not the newest, largest, heaviest, or even the most accurate missiles in Iran’s arsenal, but they are produced in large quantities and are effective at what they do (direct strikes and use in group attacks to distract enemy air defenses by passing faster and more powerful missiles). CENTCOM has not yet commented on Thursday night’s attacks on the USS Lincoln, but on Friday released undated photos and said the carrier "continues to operate, both day and night." The Gerald R. Ford carrier, accompanying this warship, was put out of action in mid-March after a "mysterious fire in the laundry room" and had to go to Crete for repairs. Some observers say this ship may have been targeted by Iranian missiles.

OSINTdefender An Iranian one-way drone attack last month on the U.S. Embassy in Saudi Arabia’s capital of Riyadh did more extensive damage than previously disclosed, penetrating a secure part of the embassy and heavily damaging three floors as well as a station for the Central Intelligence Agency (CIA), while sparking a blaze that raged for half a day, current and former American officials have told The New York Times. The Iranian attack on the embassy, which occurred March 3rd when an Iranian drone evaded air defenses guarding Riyadh’s gated Diplomatic Quarter and slammed into the American compound. A minute later, a second drone flew into the hole made by the first one and also exploded, the officials said. More drones were intercepted that night a few hours later, one was thought to be targeting the residence of the highest-ranking U.S. diplomat in Saudi Arabia, located a few hundred feet from the U.S. Embassy. tweet

The strike occurred overnight and officials say it could have caused mass casualties if it happened during working hours. @wfwitness
